payload.text: Vamos desenvolver um site para a Aços Vital.

**Analyzing Acros Vital's Needs**

I'm currently focused on Acros Vital. I'm visualizing a high-end React website utilizing Tailwind CSS for a polished look. I'm also planning to integrate the Gemini API, probably for a helpful product suggestion system or steel-related Q&A. This whole design will give Acros Vital a modern edge.


**Outlining the Project's Framework**

I'm solidifying the project's foundational structure. React (v18+), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and the Gemini API are the core technologies. The design ethos is industrial, modern, and trustworthy, with a steel gray, deep blue, and orange/red color palette. I'm planning bold sans-serif typography, and structuring it with key sections and features like a "Steel Consultant" AI chatbot. I'm establishing the file structure too, including a `metadata.json` for basic info.


**Refining Component Interactions**

I'm now diving into the component interactions. I'm focusing on the AI Assistant implementation, specifically using `gemini-3-flash-preview` for faster responses within `AIConsultant.tsx`. I'm also planning the Navbar as fixed and sticky, with an interactive ProductGrid. The Products section will feature interactive cards, and the AI chatbot will be a floating bubble or integrated section. The basic file structure includes an `index.html` with a Tailwind script.


**Perfecting the API Key**

I'm now zeroing in on the Gemini integration, mainly making sure the API key is securely used via `process.env.API_KEY`. The chatbot's persona will be a technical steel expert, answering detailed questions. I am also working to ensure the correct `@google/genai` pattern is used, favoring `ai.models.generateContent` instead of `GoogleGenerativeAI`.

React application entry point using React 18+ API.
Global TypeScript types and interfaces for the application.
Service for interacting with Gemini API for the technical assistant feature.
Navigation bar component with mobile responsiveness.
Hero section with high-impact visual and primary call-to-action.

entries.description: Main application component assembling all sections of the landing page.

payload.text:
**Updating Website Structure**

I'm currently focused on incorporating the user's HTML structure into the Aços Vital website's `index.html` file. My priority is integrating the provided meta tags for enhanced SEO, Open Graph, and Twitter card support. I'm also configuring the Tailwind CSS framework with the new color palette they specified.


**Refining Visual Style**

I'm now implementing the new color palette and typography across the website. Specifically, I'm updating the Tailwind CSS configuration with the new brand colors like `brand-blue-dark` and `brand-blue-secondary`, and I'm updating the font to Inter. The import map is being updated, too, using `aistudiocdn.com` for React and Lucide. I'm also adding global styles. I will then refactor components to use these utilities.


**Adapting Styles to Components**

I'm now fully immersed in updating the component styles to align with the new brand guidelines. I'm focusing on incorporating the color palette (`brand-blue-dark`, `brand-blue-secondary`, `brand-orange`, and `brand-whatsapp`) and the background color in all of the existing components, from the Navbar and Hero sections to the ProductCard and the Footer. I've also noted that I'll need to remove the reference to `index.css` as it's not needed. Finally, I've updated the Gemini Service, and I need to add categories for Flanges and Connections.


**Revising Implementation Strategy**

I've just reconsidered the CSS implementation. Now, I'll provide a minimal `index.css` file to prevent 404 errors, handling potential custom scrollbars. Furthermore, since the user's HTML uses `lucide-react`, I'll switch icon components from Font Awesome for improved performance and a modern look. The existing import map from `index.html` via `aistudiocdn.com` will be kept as specified.


**Implementing Tailwinds Config**

I'm now fully immersed in updating the Tailwind configuration. I've added the custom color definitions, including `brand-orange` as `#FF751A` and `brand-blue-dark` as `#081437`. I'm verifying the inclusion of the Tailwind CDN script and ensuring the correct path for `index.html`. Furthermore, I've checked `index.tsx` for changes, confirming the import map update, and will now implement the user's requirement to switch to `lucide-react` icons.

entries.description: Update index.html with new SEO meta tags, brand colors in Tailwind config, and new import maps for React 19 and Lucide icons.
Add new product categories: Flanges, Connections, and Valves to match the new business scope.
Update Gemini system instructions to include the expanded product scope and emphasize the new branding.
Update Navbar to use brand-blue-dark, brand-orange, and Lucide icons.
Update Hero section with new brand colors and background imagery.

payload.text:
**Initiating Code Integration**

I'm currently working on incorporating the new HTML structure into the Aços Vital website. My primary focus is on ensuring the `index.tsx` file correctly manages and initializes the i18n setup. This is a crucial step for the successful integration of the code changes.


**Integrating Website Elements**

I've made considerable progress on integrating the new HTML structure. I'm focused on populating the necessary files now. I've created the `index.css` file as per the instructions and I've also examined the provided `index.html` file, which includes detailed SEO meta tags, brand colors in the Tailwind config, and a specific import map for the project. I'm now making the corresponding changes to `index.tsx` as well.


**Prioritizing Icon Compatibility**

I'm now focused on the Lucide icon integration. I've realized the `lucide-react` version in the import map has changed, so I need to update icon usages accordingly to ensure compatibility. The provided `index.tsx` snippet also requires a new `lib/i18n.ts` file. I'll create this file with a basic setup. I'll make sure to use the provided imports.


**Verifying Project Directory Structure**

I'm now making sure the project directory structure aligns with the given instructions, especially regarding file paths. The user's `index.html` file includes references to `/index.css` and `/index.tsx`, so I'll adjust the paths in my code accordingly. Also, the HTML's `lucide-react` version matches my previous usage. Furthermore, the provided brand colors continue to align, so there are no changes to be made there.


**Resolving File Discrepancies**

I'm now focusing on discrepancies between the initial and updated HTML structures. I'll need to examine the "SEGUE O NOVO HTML" section carefully. I'll ensure I provide `lib/i18n.ts`, as indicated in `index.tsx`. I'll also add `index.css` for completeness. I need to make sure the project directory is up to date based on the newly provided HTML structure.


**Updating Import Maps**

I'm now focusing on the import map provided by the user and ensuring my code aligns with the specified dependencies. I'll make sure to integrate the versions: "react": "https://esm.sh/react@^18.2.0", "react-dom": "https://esm.sh/react-dom@^18.2.0", "react-router-dom": "https://esm.sh/react-router-dom@^6.22.1", "i18next": "https://esm.sh/i18next@^24.1.1", "i18next-browser-languagedetector": "https://esm.sh/i18next-browser-languagedetector@^7.2.0", "recharts": "https://esm.sh/recharts@^3.6.0". I'm also preparing to handle the user's `index.tsx` file and confirm the file structure.


**Implementing React Structure**

I'm now integrating the provided React structure. I'm focusing on incorporating the user's `index.tsx` file, ensuring the root element handling is correct, and I'll include the `lib/i18n.ts` file as per instructions. This will include initializing i18next configuration. I'll make sure to create the file and add a basic setup.


**Generating Updated Files**

I'm now preparing the updated files based on the newest user-provided information. I'll make sure to retain the Inter font and accessibility utilities from the provided `<style>` block. I will also incorporate the `src` and `link` tags for the corresponding `.tsx` and `.css` files. I'm focusing on aligning the structure with the latest user specifications. I will take into account the necessary metadata.json details as well.

entries.description: Atualização do HTML com meta tags de SEO, configuração de cores da marca no Tailwind e import maps para React 19.
Atualização do entry point para incluir a inicialização do i18n conforme solicitado.
Configuração inicial do i18next para suporte a múltiplos idiomas e detecção automática.
Arquivo CSS principal para diretivas Tailwind e estilos globais.

payload.text:
**Deleting Gemini Integration**

I've made progress on removing the Gemini integration. The `AIConsultant.tsx` component is gone. Next, I'm taking out the `geminiService.ts` file, a key step. Lastly, I'll update `App.tsx` to remove the now-unnecessary `AIConsultant` import, which is straightforward. I'm focusing on the cleanup phase now.


**Refining Component Removals**

I'm now refining the component removals. I've updated `App.tsx`, `Navbar.tsx`, and `Hero.tsx` to reflect the changes. I'm focusing on the component and link removals, ensuring the UI no longer references the Gemini integration. The `ChatMessage` type is under review to see if it's Gemini-specific. My goal is a clean codebase post-removal.


**Updating Main Entry Points**

I'm now focusing on updating the main entry points. I've removed the `ChatMessage` type from `types.ts`, streamlining data structures. I'm ensuring `App.tsx` no longer imports or uses `AIConsultant`, which is now irrelevant. This refactoring includes removing any dependencies that might still exist on the Gemini integration within the app's core components.


**Implementing Final Clean-up**

I'm now fully immersed in the final clean-up. I've removed the Nav link in `Navbar.tsx` and the CTA button in `Hero.tsx`. I've also removed the now-unused `ChatMessage` type from `types.ts`, and checked for remaining references. Further, I've scrubbed the `@google/genai` import from `index.html`. My focus is on completely eliminating all traces of the integration.

entries.description: Remoção da interface ChatMessage não utilizada.
Atualização da descrição do app no metadados.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Type Errors**

I'm currently focused on resolving the "ChatMessage" type import errors. It seems like both `geminiService.ts` and `AIConsultant.tsx` require a `ChatMessage` type, which is not defined in `types.ts` as it only defines `Product`. I need to figure out how to best define this missing type, or perhaps there's a misconfiguration in the import paths.


**Resolving Type Mismatch**

I've added the `ChatMessage` interface to `types.ts`, which should clear the initial import errors. Now I'm shifting focus to `geminiService.ts`. I need to initialize `GoogleGenAI` correctly with the API key. After checking the `@google/genai` guidelines, I've realized the `history` parameter is likely misapplied for `generateContent`, so I need to check its purpose to ensure the correct usage with the updated prompt and system instructions. I'm focusing on aligning the code with the library's `generateContent` method.


**Debugging Gemini Interaction**

I'm now diving into the `geminiService.ts` file again. I've confirmed `response.text` is the correct property to access the text. I am also aware the Google GenAI library is imported as `import { GoogleGenAI } from "@google/genai";`. I will now look to ensure proper response handling.


**Evaluating API Alternatives**

I am now focusing on the API alternatives for handling chat history. While `generateContent` might not be ideal for multi-turn chats, the documentation for `ai.chats.create` seems better suited, although I am still considering how to best handle the transition from the current implementation. I need to figure out how to map the existing `messages` and `userMsg` within `AIConsultant.tsx` to align with the `ai.chats.create` method and its expected `history` parameter.


**Refining Message Structure**

I'm now revising my approach to align with the provided guidelines strictly, and to get rid of type errors. I've corrected the Gemini SDK initialization in `geminiService.ts`, ensuring direct API key usage. Now, I'm verifying the `generateContent` usage aligns with the documented single-turn chat approach. I need to make the single-turn chat, which should resolve the immediate error.


**Optimizing Gemini Integration**

I'm now revising the Gemini SDK integration in `geminiService.ts` to strictly adhere to the documented guidelines, focusing on `ai.models.generateContent`. The immediate goal is to replace the current `generateContent` method with the proper API usage. I'll make sure to provide the model name directly within the `ai.models.generateContent` call, along with the prompt, in a compliant way. I will now look to ensure proper history handling if available, keeping the focus on compliance.


**Revising Contextual Knowledge**

payload.text:
**Analyzing Aços Vital Sync**

I'm currently focused on synchronizing the provided `types.ts` file with the existing `Aços Vital` application structure. I'm carefully analyzing the provided code to identify and address any implicit requirements or potential discrepancies between the files to ensure seamless integration and functionality.


**Merging Type Definitions**

I'm currently merging the original `types.ts` with the user's latest update. It seems a "Steel Weight Calculator" feature is being added. I'm carefully comparing the existing `Product` interface, noting the subtle differences (like `image` vs. `imageUrl`), and planning how best to incorporate the new calculation-related types (`SteelType`, `ShapeType`, `CalculationResult`) without breaking any current functionality. I also plan to reconcile any discrepancies and potential conflicts, especially in components like `App.tsx` and `ProductCard.tsx`.


**Refining Data Structures**

I'm now revising the `types.ts` definitions. I need to update the `Product` interface to use `imageUrl` and `specs` and make changes throughout. I'll retain `SteelCategory` and also add new types for the weight calculator and `ChatMessage` improvements. Following this, I plan to integrate the new `WeightCalculator` into the main application page.


**Updating Components and Interfaces**

I'm now implementing the `WeightCalculator.tsx` component, as instructed. I'm focusing on its visual integration, specifically using the brand colors as specified. Furthermore, I'll update the `Product` interface to reflect the use of `imageUrl` and `specs`, as the code requires, ensuring consistency. Finally, I'll review and integrate the updated `ChatMessage` handling in `AIConsultant.tsx`. I noticed a discrepancy. I will prioritize the provided code, as the source of truth, but note the "Remove Gemini" request.


**Resolving Contradictions**

I'm now grappling with a contradiction. The user previously requested removal of Gemini, but the provided list of existing files includes `AIConsultant.tsx` and `geminiService.ts`. I'm choosing to prioritize the current list of files, but will keep the user's removal request in mind. The new `types.ts` file takes precedence. I'll focus on integrating those updates.


**Developing New Calculator Feature**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the new weight calculator feature, taking full account of existing files. I'm prioritizing integrating the `types.ts` updates and building the interactive `WeightCalculator.tsx` component. The `Product` interface will be adjusted to incorporate the `imageUrl` and `specs` changes as well. This will ensure seamless integration with the catalog. I'm using the requested brand colors and Lucide icons. I'm focusing on responsiveness and visual integration.


**Maintaining File Consistency**

I'm now focusing on reconciling the provided "existing files" with the newly provided `types.ts`. I need to be diligent about keeping Gemini if the files require it, alongside the calculator. The latest `types.ts` is significantly different from previous versions, which means I'll need to carefully merge the `SteelCategory` enum back into the updated structure to preserve functionality in `App.tsx` and the corresponding filters. I need to make sure the app still correctly uses: `SHEETS`, `TUBES`, `BARS`, `PROFILES`, `FLANGES`, `CONNECTIONS`, `VALVES`.

Para complementar o site da **Aços Vital** com as novas definições de tipos e a funcionalidade de calculadora de peso (sugerida pelos tipos `CalculationResult`, `SteelType` e `ShapeType`), realizei as seguintes atualizações:

1.  **Sincronização do `types.ts`**: Atualizei a interface `Product` (campos `imageUrl` e `specs`) e adicionei os enums de materiais e formatos.
2.  **Criação da Calculadora de Peso**: Um novo componente interativo que permite aos usuários calcular o peso teórico de chapas e tubos, essencial para o setor siderúrgico.
3.  **Refatoração do Catálogo**: Ajustei o `App.tsx` e o `ProductCard.tsx` para utilizarem os novos nomes de campos da interface `Product`.
4.  **Integração Visual**: A nova calculadora segue a identidade visual de alta performance, utilizando o azul marinho e o laranja vibrante da marca.

entries.description: Atualização completa dos tipos conforme solicitado, incluindo suporte para calculadora de peso e metadados de chat.
Novo componente de Calculadora de Peso Teórico para auxiliar clientes técnicos.
Ajuste dos campos Product (imageUrl e specs) para compatibilidade com o novo types.ts.
Integração da nova seção Calculadora e ajuste do catálogo para os novos campos da interface Product.
Ajuste no AIConsultant para suportar o novo timestamp no ChatMessage.

payload.text:
**Considering Routing Complexity**

I'm currently evaluating the implications of incorporating a more sophisticated routing mechanism, inspired by the `App.tsx` snippet provided. My focus is on understanding the trade-offs of the current direct component rendering versus a custom routing solution, and how the changes will impact the overall structure and maintainability. I have been focused on a custom routing solution as the next step.


**Adapting to Proposed Changes**

I'm now integrating the user's `App.tsx` snippet, focusing on implementing `useRouter` and `React.lazy` for code splitting, as suggested. I see the need to create the required route components like `HomePage` and `FlangeProductPage`. The previous direct component rendering in `App.tsx` is being replaced with a modular routing approach. I'm actively assessing how to best adapt the existing content to fit this updated architecture and improve maintainability.


**Implementing Specific Routes**

I'm now focused on the concrete implementation, using the structure of the user's `App.tsx` file as a blueprint. I'm prioritizing the creation of essential route components like `HomePage`, `TablesPage`, and `CalculatorPage` from existing content. I'm also renaming and moving components for consistency, and creating `hooks/useRouter.ts`. The implementation approach focuses on the route structure provided by the user, and uses `React.lazy` and a `routes` map. I'm making progress.


**Clarifying User Intent**

I need to determine the scope of the user's request. Based on the prompt, I believe I need to update the application to use the provided `App.tsx` file, rather than simply fixing the existing version. The new `App.tsx` references non-existent files. I will update `App.tsx` and create the required components to avoid crashes and ensure functionality.


**Establishing Key Components**

I'm now focused on building the foundational components and pages, guided by the user's requirements and the structure of `App.tsx`. I'll create `LoadingSpinner`, `CookieBanner`, `HomePage`, `TablesPage`, and `CalculatorPage`. I'm starting by setting up `hooks/useRouter.ts` and refining the route structure for consistency, with `TablesPage` as the primary table display and `HomePage` housing the Hero section and general information.


**Structuring Necessary Components**

I'm now focusing on creating foundational components and pages based on the user's `App.tsx` structure. I'm prioritizing the `LoadingSpinner` and `CookieBanner` components within the `common` folder, and the `Header` and `Footer` in the `layout` directory. I'll need to make sure the header height is aligned with the `pt-[114px] lg:pt-[124px]` from `App.tsx`. Next, I'll flesh out pages like `HomePage`, `TablesPage`, and `CalculatorPage`, alongside creating `hooks/useRouter.ts`. Finally, I'll address subpages like `ProductsPage` and `CatalogPage` as well.


**Refining Component Assignments**

I'm now prioritizing the components from the user's `App.tsx` file to specific pages. My plan is to move `Hero` to `HomePage`, `WeightCalculator` to `CalculatorPage`, and `Contact` to `ContactPage`. `ProductCard` will remain as a reusable component within `ProductsPage`. I'll also ensure all imports within `App.tsx` work, addressing any pathing issues. I am going to make the new app *not* crash, and look good, with the updated routing.


**Creating Enhanced Header**

I'm now focusing on the `Header` component, and it seems this should encompass both a `TopBar` and `MainBar`. The old `Navbar` had a height of 64px, and after padding, it fits the user's requirement. I'll need to account for heights specified in `App.tsx`, and I'm updating pages like `ProductsPage`, `CatalogPage`, and other product subpages to show I can handle the new structure.


**Developing Foundational Elements**

I'm now prioritizing the basic components. I've focused on `LoadingSpinner` as a centered CSS spinner, and `CookieBanner` as a fixed banner at the bottom. I am also aiming to maintain the "brand-blue-dark" and "brand-orange" styling, with high-quality industrial imagery. I'm working with the new `useRouter.ts` from the user, as well as the `Header` layout.


**Refining Header Functionality**

I'm now implementing the `Header` component from scratch, incorporating scroll-based styling and a mobile menu. I am working on the scroll functionality with `useState` and `useEffect` to manage header visibility, along with handling the mobile menu state. The header includes a top bar with contact details and a main bar for navigation, both responsive to screen size.


**Constructing Navigation Links**

I'm presently building the navigation links within the `Header` component. I am focusing on the user's `App.tsx` navigation structure, which includes the home route, tables, catalog, and LGPD. My attention is on implementing hover effects, and ensuring proper navigation paths using anchor links for a single-page application experience. I have to work with both mobile and desktop menus, and ensure the right structure.



**Developing Products Navigation**

I'm now integrating the provided `App.tsx` navigation for the product section. I'm building out the dropdown menu for products, handling hover states and sub-navigation links. I need to get the "Produtos" dropdown working, with subpages like "Aços", "Tubos", and "Conexões". I'm also addressing responsive design for both desktop and mobile views. The desktop view will use a dropdown on hover, and the mobile view must accommodate a separate, responsive menu.



**Building Product Navigation**

I'm now focusing on integrating the product navigation, implementing the provided product sub-navigation within the header. I'm prioritizing the creation of the dropdown menu for products, specifically handling hover effects. I am getting the dropdown working, ensuring it displays "Chapas", "Tubos", "Flanges", and "Conexões". I also need to make sure the menu works for both desktop and mobile views. The menu must be responsive.


**Constructing Mobile Menu**

I am now creating a mobile menu, which needs to use a "hamburger" menu icon. I need to make sure the menu works for smaller screens. I am focusing on the navigation links from `App.tsx` and handling the display states to switch between menu and the X close icon. I am targeting responsive design for this component, making sure it works seamlessly on various devices. The menu will display on click.



**Implementing Mobile Menu**

I'm now implementing the mobile menu functionality in the `Header` component, with the hamburger icon. I've focused on ensuring the menu's proper display on smaller screens. I am incorporating the navigation links from `App.tsx` and am managing display states using a `useState` hook to switch between the menu and the close (X) icon. Responsive design is a key priority for this feature, ensuring it works seamlessly across various devices.

Com base no novo `App.tsx` fornecido, refatorei a arquitetura do site para uma estrutura robusta de múltiplas páginas (multi-page) utilizando rotas baseadas em Hash (`#/`). 

Esta atualização inclui:
1.  **Novo Sistema de Rotas**: Implementação do hook `useRouter` para navegação dinâmica e carregamento preguiçoso (`React.lazy`) das páginas.
2.  **Arquitetura Organizada**: Separação clara entre `pages` (páginas principais), `subpages` (detalhes de produtos) e `components` (elementos reutilizáveis).
3.  **Layout Aprimorado**: Criação de um `Header` técnico com TopBar (informações de contato) e Menu Principal, além de um `Footer` institucional completo.
4.  **Páginas Técnicas**: Implementação da `TablesPage` (tabelas de peso de referência) e `CalculatorPage` (calculadora interativa), reforçando o posicionamento técnico da Aços Vital.
5.  **Componentes de UX**: Adição de `LoadingSpinner` para transições suaves e `CookieBanner` para conformidade com a LGPD.

entries.description: Implementação de um hook customizado para gerenciar o estado das rotas baseadas em hash.
Novo componente de cabeçalho com barra de contato superior e navegação principal.
Refatoração do Footer para o novo diretório de layouts.
